question.text: Under Regulation 3(1) of the SEBI (PIT) Regulations, 2015, an insider may communicate UPSI to another person if:

answer.text: Such communication is in furtherance of legitimate purposes, performance of duties, or discharge of legal obligations
The recipient is a family member of the insider
The insider obtains verbal permission from the compliance officer
The information will become public within 24 hours

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 3(1) permits communication of UPSI only in furtherance of legitimate purposes, performance of duties, or discharge of legal obligations. **The Trap:** Being a family member does not automatically entitle one to receive UPSI - it must still be for a legitimate purpose. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series III-B (Issuers Compliance) also covers the obligations of insiders regarding UPSI communication. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The board must formulate a policy defining 'legitimate purposes' as part of the Code of Fair Disclosure under Regulation 8.
